content: We can integrate Neural Turing Machine's external memory architecture into Intrinsic Curiosity Module (ICM)-based sparse reward robotic control, to store the low-reward visited state features and prediction errors from past exploration, then use the NTM's associative recall capability to avoid redundant exploration and guide the agent to actively under-explored high-potential areas. This turns unstructured sparse reward exploration into a structured algorithmic recall task that NTM is inherently good at processing.
source_topics: ICM-based exploration for sparse reward robotic control
trigger_topic: ICM-based exploration for sparse reward robotic control
